---
title: Working with data
---

## Add some ready-made data

## Add data from local storage

## Re-ordering data and creating groups

## Theory - Vectors and Rasters

Vector vs. Raster: Key Differences
Vector data uses math to represent individual objects, while Raster data uses a grid of pixels to represent a continuous area.

Choose Vector when precision and specific location data are key. It is the standard for urban planning, navigation, and boundary mapping.

Choose Raster for broad environmental analysis. It is the best choice for satellite imagery, terrain modeling, and scientific modeling (like predicting floods).

In a real QGIS project, you will almost always use both together. For example, you might place a Vector layer of site locations on top of a Raster satellite image to see how they align with the terrain.

![The difference between Vector and Rasters](../figs/GISI/Working_With_Files/Raster_Vector.png)
